text: Year of the World 3107, Year before Christ 897.
Affinity, taking Achab's daughter for his son's wife. (Tirinus) (Chap. 21:6., and 3 Kings xxii.)

War. Josaphat was too complaisant. He was rebuked by the son of Hanani, as Asa had been by his father; (chap. 16:7.) and, not long after, Eliezer reproached him for joining in alliance with Ochozias, 2 Paralipomenon 20. (Calmet)<hr>He acted without an ill intention, and was more easily pardoned. (Worthington)
Hands. He ironically (Calmet) alludes to the declarations of the false prophets. (Haydock)
Ye. In 3 Kings, he addresses his discourse chiefly to Achab. (Calmet)
Said. See 3 Kings xxii. The Rabbins pretend that the spirit of Naboth came forth. But it was satan, or an evil spirit. (Tirinus)

So. The Lord permits evil, but he does not approve of it. (Calmet)<hr>Homer makes Jupiter send a deluding dream to Agamemnon. (Iliad B.)<hr>The delusive predictions of impostors, are one great source of calamities. (Du Hamel)
Amelech. Septuagint and Syriac, "to Joas, the commander, son of the father. (Haydock)<hr>He had perhaps the government of affairs at Samaria, during his king." Achab's absence. (Tirinus)
People. Mark what I say. The event will quickly prove whether I am a prophet or an impostor. (Haydock)
Dress. Protestants, "disguised himself, and they went." See 3 Kings 22:30.
Turned. Hebrew, "moved them from him." They felt that he was under God's protection; and discovered that he was not the king of Israel. (Haydock)
